name: Horace Heidt
abstract: Horace Heidt (May 21, 1901 – December 1, 1986) was an American pianist, big band leader, and radio and television personality. His band, Horace Heidt and his Musical Knights, toured vaudeville and performed on radio and television during the 1930s and 1940s.

concerts.label: Benefit: Jewish Community Center of Saranac Lake

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: Tony Johnson, Tony Ginsanti, he went with Horace Hite, and then with Frankie Carle, when Frankie Carle left Horace Heidt, he went with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Palmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 24, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: We did the ABC Dance Party, where we imitated all the bands, Horace Heidt, Glen Gray, Count Basie, Duke Ellington.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Kay
about_quotes.evidence: Shorty Sherock was a great trumpet player. He played with Horace Heidt.
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001
